# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a simple GitHub Actions workflow for our application CI. On every push, install dependencies, run unit tests, build the application, and deploy to our staging environment. Focus on speed - we want fast feedback for developers. We'll add security scanning later.

Prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD workflow with GitHub Actions
on: [push]
jobs:
  build_and_test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2.3.1 # Change to 'actions/setup-node' for setup nodejs version in your CI environment if needed 
        
      - name: Install dependencies (including NodeJS)
        run: |
          sudo apt update && \
          curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/creationix/nvm/v0.34.0/install.sh | bash
          source ~/.nvm/nvm.sh  # This line is to setup NodeJS version in your CI environment if needed, you can change it according to the requirement of each project or team member's nodejs versions  
          npm install -g create-react-app@latest && \
          cd ${{github.workspace}}/myAppName &&  # replace 'myAppName' with your app name and make sure you have a react application ready to be built here, if not then comment out this line or use the command npm install in it as well  
      - name: Run unit tests (if any)
        run: |
          cd ${{github.workspace}}/myAppName &&  # replace 'myAppName' with your app name and make sure you have a react application ready to be built here, if not then comment out this line or use the command npm test in it as well  
      - name: Build Application (if any)
        run: |
          cd ${{github.workspace}}/myAppName &&  # replace 'myAppName' with your app name and make sure you have a react application ready to be built here, if not then comment out this line or use the command npm build in it as well  
      - name: Deploy Application (if any)
        run: |
          cd ${{github.workspace}}/myAppName &&  # replace 'myAppName' with your app name and make sure you have a react application ready to be deployed here, if not then comment out this line or use the command npm deploy in it as well
